timing_config.note: All models timed in a single pass at end of script. torch.cuda.synchronize() before/after timed window. Mask-based methods have identical compute graphs to baseline — latency differences reflect measurement variance only.

paired_comparisons.threshold_scope_no_ft: 1a vs 7  → global vs per-layer threshold (both: no ft)
paired_comparisons.threshold_scope_with_ft: 1b vs 3  → global vs per-layer threshold (both: ft=Nep)
paired_comparisons.fine_tune_value: 7  vs 3  → no ft vs ft=Nep (both: per-layer L1)
paired_comparisons.criterion_quality: 3  vs 4  → L1 vs Taylor (both: per-layer, ft=Nep, same mask enforcement)
paired_comparisons.pruning_schedule: 3  vs 6  → one-shot vs iterative (both: per-layer L1, ft=Nep)
paired_comparisons.ft_value_global: 1a vs 1b → ft effect on global-pruned model

design_principles.P1_one_variable: Each paired comparison isolates exactly one variable. All other axes are controlled.
design_principles.P2_budget_control: All ft methods use exactly 10 total epochs @ lr=0.001. Iterative: [4, 3, 3] per round (sum=10). Iterative LR is constant (0.001) — no decay (FIX-F).
design_principles.P3_lth_parity: LTH θ_T trained 50 epochs with lr=0.1, SGD, CosineAnnealingLR, ColorJitter — identical to baseline (FIX-C). Retrain 50 epochs with frozen mask.
design_principles.P4_mask_scope: LTH masks Conv2d+Linear weights only. BN affine excluded (small by normalization dynamics, not unimportance).
design_principles.P5_timing: All models timed in dedicated end-of-script pass with CUDA sync.
design_principles.P6_augmentation_parity: transform_train includes ColorJitter (brightness=0.2, contrast=0.2, saturation=0.2) — matches baseline augmentation exactly (FIX-B).
design_principles.P7_optimizer_parity: train_model uses SGD(lr=0.1, momentum=0.9, wd=5e-4) + CosineAnnealingLR — matches baseline optimizer exactly. fine_tune uses FINETUNE_LR=1e-3 (lower LR, standard for recovery).

complete_fix_history.v3_FIX2_iterative_sparsity: make_permanent() outside loop; sparsity reaches target.
complete_fix_history.v3_FIX3_taylor_naming: Method 4 = Taylor Sensitivity, not Movement Pruning.
complete_fix_history.v4_FIXA_magnitude_ft: Method 3 fine-tunes; was identical to Method 7.
complete_fix_history.v4_FIXB_lth_bn_mask: LTH masks conv+linear only; BN rewound freely.
complete_fix_history.v5_FIXII_budget_control: All ft methods use exactly 10 total epochs.
complete_fix_history.v5_FIXIII_lth_parity_doc: LTH training budget documented vs baseline.
complete_fix_history.v5_FIXV_method1b: Method 1b (global+ft) added for clean threshold comparison.
complete_fix_history.v7_FIXA_baseline_epochs: BASELINE_EPOCHS corrected to 50 (was 100 in v6, mismatching actual baseline checkpoint trained for 50 epochs).
complete_fix_history.v7_FIXB_colorjitter: ColorJitter added to transform_train (brightness=0.2, contrast=0.2, saturation=0.2) matching baseline augmentation. Previously pruning fine-tuning and LTH retraining lacked ColorJitter, biasing all pruned models against the baseline.
complete_fix_history.v7_FIXC_lth_trajectory: LTH θ_T now trained 50 epochs (was 100) with lr=0.1, SGD, CosineAnnealingLR, ColorJitter — identical to baseline. Previously LTH used a different epoch count and augmentation, so the winning ticket did not reflect the true baseline loss landscape.
complete_fix_history.v7_FIXD_iterative_threshold: Method 6 now uses per-layer l1_unstructured (not global_unstructured). Previously the 3 vs 6 comparison conflated schedule with threshold scope. Now the only variable is pruning schedule (one-shot vs iterative).
complete_fix_history.v7_FIXE_taylor_hook: Method 4 now uses TaylorUnstructured custom prune hook. The binary mask is enforced on every forward pass during fine-tuning via the weight_orig/weight_mask reparametrization — same mechanism as l1_unstructured. Previously p.mul_() zeroed weights once, allowing fine-tuning to refill them (unfair vs Method 3).
complete_fix_history.v7_FIXF_iterative_lr: Method 6 fine-tune LR is now constant (0.001) across all rounds. Previously LR decayed as FINETUNE_LR * 0.5^r, adding an extra confound to the 3 vs 6 schedule comparison.

_meta.flops_note: Mask-based: dense FLOPs unchanged (graph unmodified). Structured (#2): GFLOPs genuinely reduced, measured on rebuilt model. Cross-type FLOPs comparison is not apples-to-apples.
_meta.structured_pruning_safety: Only internal Bottleneck width (conv1→conv2) is pruned. conv3, bn3, downsample untouched — residual shape safety guaranteed.

1a_unstructured_noft.flops_note: Mask-based pruning — computation graph unchanged. Dense FLOPs identical to baseline. Effective FLOPs require sparse-kernel backend (e.g. DeepSparse).
1a_unstructured_noft.method_note: Global L1 unstructured, no fine-tuning. Paired with 7_one_shot (per-layer threshold, no ft) to isolate threshold scope. Paired with 1b_unstructured_ft (same pruning, adds ft) to isolate ft value.

1b_unstructured_ft.flops_note: Mask-based pruning — computation graph unchanged. Dense FLOPs identical to baseline. Effective FLOPs require sparse-kernel backend (e.g. DeepSparse).
1b_unstructured_ft.method_note: Global L1 unstructured + 10-epoch fine-tune @ lr=0.001. Paired with 1a (isolates ft value) and 3_magnitude (isolates threshold scope).

2_structured.flops_note: TRUE structured pruning: 30% of internal Bottleneck channels physically removed (conv1+bn1+conv2 per block). conv3, bn3, downsample untouched — residual shapes guaranteed. GFLOPs genuinely reduced; measured by thop on rebuilt model.

3_magnitude.flops_note: Mask-based pruning — computation graph unchanged. Dense FLOPs identical to baseline. Effective FLOPs require sparse-kernel backend (e.g. DeepSparse).
3_magnitude.method_note: Per-layer L1 magnitude pruning + 10-epoch fine-tune @ lr=0.001. Han et al. 2015. Central reference method.

4_taylor_sensitivity.flops_note: Mask-based pruning — computation graph unchanged. Dense FLOPs identical to baseline. Effective FLOPs require sparse-kernel backend (e.g. DeepSparse).
4_taylor_sensitivity.method_note: Taylor first-order sensitivity (Molchanov et al. 2017). Importance = (1/N) Σ |∂L/∂w × w| over N=391 batches, normalized. Mask registered via TaylorUnstructured hook (same mechanism as l1_unstructured). Fine-tuned 10 epochs @ lr=0.001 (identical to Method 3). NOT movement pruning (Sanh et al. 2020).

5_lottery_ticket.flops_note: Mask-based pruning — computation graph unchanged. Dense FLOPs identical to baseline. Effective FLOPs require sparse-kernel backend (e.g. DeepSparse).
5_lottery_ticket.lth_note: LTH — Frankle & Carlin 2019. θ₀ @ SEED=42. θ_T: 50-epoch in-script training (lr=0.1, SGD, ColorJitter) matching baseline trajectory exactly (FIX-C). Global magnitude mask @ 50% on Conv2d+Linear weights only (P4). BN affine rewound to θ₀, retrained freely. Ticket retrained 50 epochs with frozen mask.

6_iterative.flops_note: Mask-based pruning — computation graph unchanged. Dense FLOPs identical to baseline. Effective FLOPs require sparse-kernel backend (e.g. DeepSparse).
6_iterative.method_note: 3-round per-layer L1 iterative pruning (FIX-D: per-layer, not global). Per-round sparsity: 0.2063 (compounds to 50%). Fine-tune epochs: [4, 3, 3] per round (total=10). Fine-tune LR: 0.001 constant across rounds (FIX-F: no decay). Controlled budget: total ft = Methods 3 and 4 (10 ep). make_permanent() called once after all rounds (masks AND-accumulate).

7_one_shot.flops_note: Mask-based pruning — computation graph unchanged. Dense FLOPs identical to baseline. Effective FLOPs require sparse-kernel backend (e.g. DeepSparse).
7_one_shot.method_note: Per-layer L1, 50% sparsity, zero fine-tuning. Paired with 1a (global threshold, no ft) → isolates threshold scope. Paired with 3 (same criterion, ft=Nep) → isolates fine-tuning value.
